title: Canonical Interview Question
description: One canonical record per question. One file per question lives at interview_questions_knowledge/questions/<category>/<question_id>.json. The Card Data Schema rendered by the dashboard is derived from this record at HTML build time (Loop 8).

question_id.description: Stable canonical slug (e.g. 'two-sum', 'design-url-shortener'). Used as the filename and as the join key in evidence/, aliases.json, and runs/<ts>/diff.json.

category.description: Category slug. The six seed categories below are always valid. Additional categories may be discovered by Loop 5 (see 05-replacement.md, 'Dynamic category discovery') and registered in interview_questions_knowledge/categories.json. Any registered category slug matching the pattern is valid.

subcategory.description: DSA pattern (Arrays & Hashing, Graphs, DP) or system-design domain (Caching, Messaging) or behavioral theme (Conflict, Ownership).

rank.description: Rank within category (1-based). Set by Loop 8 sort, not stored permanently.

count.description: Times the company is reported asking this question across confirmed evidence.

level_tags.description: Levels this question is reported at for this company (e.g. 'L4','L5','SDE2'). See policies/company_level_tags.json for taxonomy.

thesis.description: Evidence summary explaining why this question is ranked here.

sources.description: De-duplicated list of source domains that confirm this question. Provenance details live in evidence/<question_id>.jsonl.

risks.description: Counterarguments and weaknesses surfaced by Loop 4 adversarial pass.

evidence_count.description: Total number of evidence records in evidence/<question_id>.jsonl. Recomputed by Loop 2.

independent_source_count.description: After provenance/citation-cycle dedup. This is the value Loop 2 uses for the '2+ independent Tier 1/2 sources' rule.

newest_recent_year.description: Newest report year from a Tier 1/2 source within the rolling Y / Y-1 / Y-2 recency window. null if no recent confirmation.

first_seen_cycle.description: Cycle number when this question first entered the candidate set.

last_confirmed_cycle.description: Most recent cycle in which Loop 2 re-confirmed this question.

score_history.description: Append one entry per cycle to track trajectory.

adversarial_passes.description: Count of Loop 4 passes this question has survived. Required >= 2 for convergence.

prep.description: Concise 'how to answer' study layer generated by Loop 9 (09-answer-synthesis.md). NOT a full solution — just the scaffolding needed to recognize and produce a strong answer. Grounded in the evidence quotes already gathered, so it stays factual rather than hallucinated.

key_points.description: 3-6 bullet talking points capturing the crux of a strong answer. Coding: the optimal idea + why. System design: the must-mention components. Behavioral: the STAR beats to hit. Conceptual: the core claims.

approach.description: One short paragraph. Coding: optimal technique + time/space complexity (e.g. 'Hash map, one pass, O(n)/O(n)'). System design: components, data flow, key tradeoffs. Behavioral: which STAR angle + which company value/principle it maps to. Conceptual: the model answer in 2-4 sentences.

common_mistakes.description: 1-4 traps interviewees commonly fall into on this question.

followups.description: Typical interviewer follow-ups, so the user can prepare a layer deeper.

confidence.description: Loop 9's confidence that these points are well-grounded in the evidence. Low values are surfaced as a caveat in the dashboard.

generated_in_cycle.description: Cycle in which this prep content was last (re)generated.

behavioral_signals.description: Populated only for swe-behavioral category. See 03-scoring.md for the alternate matrix.

answer_archetype.description: STAR archetype (Conflict, Ownership, Failure, Ambiguity, etc.).
